skill.name: evaluation
skill.description: Build evaluation frameworks for agent systems, including rubrics, test sets, quality gates, and production monitoring.
skill.summary: Design and run structured evaluations for agent quality and reliability.
skill.author: muratcankoylan

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, sensitive key access, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are false positives from Markdown code fences, numeric scoring examples, dictionary keys, and evaluation terminology; no malicious intent, prompt injection, network activity, subprocess use, or secret access was found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Command Execution Alerts
low_findings.description: The flagged backtick locations are Markdown code fences and Python examples. No subprocess, shell execution, or user-controlled command construction was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static pattern exists, but semantic review shows documentation fences and sample Python only. There is no executable shell invocation in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Alerts
low_findings.description: The flagged weak cryptography locations refer to rubric scores, documentation text, or model names. No cryptographic algorithm or hashing implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines do not contain MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic operations. The detector appears to have matched unrelated evaluation terminology and numeric score examples.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Sensitive Key Access Alerts
low_findings.description: The flagged key locations use dictionary keys for rubric dimensions. No certificate files, private keys, or secret material were accessed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The word keys is used as a collection method, not as credential handling. No evidence of secret file access was found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Reconnaissance Alerts
low_findings.description: The flagged reconnaissance locations describe agent evaluation concepts and internal skill references. No host, network, or service discovery behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text discusses tool use and evaluation domains, not system or network enumeration. No reconnaissance commands or network calls were found.

content.value_statement: Agent systems are hard to test because valid paths and outputs can vary. This skill helps teams build rubrics, test sets, quality gates, and monitoring loops.
actual_capabilities: Defines multi-dimensional rubrics for factual accuracy, completeness, citations, source quality, and tool efficiency.
Explains how to build test sets across simple, medium, complex, and very complex tasks.
Provides a Python evaluation framework with scoring, test set management, runners, and monitoring helpers.
Guides LLM-as-judge and human review workflows for agent output quality.
Supports regression testing and quality gates for agent pipeline changes.
Describes production sampling and pass-rate monitoring for deployed agents.
limitations: The Python evaluator uses demonstration heuristics, not a complete production judge.
It does not provide ready-made datasets for specific domains.
Human review is still needed for subtle failures, bias, and high-risk decisions.
It does not integrate directly with external monitoring platforms.

use_cases.title: Validate Agent Releases
use_cases.description: Create repeatable quality gates before shipping new prompts, tools, or model changes.

use_cases.title: Design Evaluation Rubrics
use_cases.description: Define weighted quality dimensions and scoring levels for agent outputs.

use_cases.title: Monitor Production Quality
use_cases.description: Sample live interactions and track pass rates, average scores, and alerts.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Rubric
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new agent evaluation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple evaluation rubric for my agent. Include factual accuracy, completeness, citations, source quality, and tool efficiency.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Test Set
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate test design for recurring checks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a test set for this agent workflow. Include simple, medium, complex, and very complex cases with expected outcomes.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Agent Versions
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced regression review across model or prompt changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these two agent versions using a weighted rubric. Identify quality changes, regressions, and release risks.

prompt_templates.title: Design Production Monitoring
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced quality tracking for deployed systems.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a production evaluation plan for my agent. Include sampling, pass-rate thresholds, alerting, human review, and trend reporting.

output_examples.input: Evaluate whether my research agent is ready for release.
output_examples.output: A release evaluation plan with weighted rubric dimensions, representative test cases, pass thresholds, and manual review checkpoints.

output_examples.input: Create quality gates for a customer support agent.
output_examples.output: A staged quality gate process covering factual correctness, policy compliance, answer completeness, escalation behavior, and failure handling.
A monitoring plan with sampled interactions, rolling pass rates, alert thresholds, and periodic human audits.

best_practices: Evaluate outcomes and process quality instead of requiring one exact tool path.
Use small test sets during development, then expand coverage before release.
Combine automated judging with human review for high-impact workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on one overall score without checking dimension-level failures.
Do not evaluate only easy examples that mirror the development prompt.
Do not skip production monitoring after an agent passes pre-release tests.

faq.question: What does this skill help evaluate?
faq.answer: It helps evaluate agent outputs, tool use, citations, source quality, completeness, and production quality trends.

faq.question: Can it replace human review?
faq.answer: No. It recommends human review for edge cases, subtle errors, bias, and high-risk workflows.

faq.question: Does it include runnable code?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a Python demonstration evaluator for rubrics, test sets, evaluation runners, and monitoring helpers.

faq.question: Is it useful for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The evaluation concepts apply to agent workflows built with Claude, Codex, Claude Code, or similar tools.

faq.question: Does it provide domain-specific benchmarks?
faq.answer: No. You need to create test cases and expected outcomes for your own product or domain.

faq.question: How should I start using it?
faq.answer: Start with a small rubric, add real user tasks, run baseline evaluations, and expand coverage around failures.
